factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in the mining and metals sector and due to reduced oil prices affecting the oil and gas segment.

evidence.text: The revenue decreases in the current year periods were principally due to a significant decline in project execution activities in the mining and metals and infrastructure business lines of the Industrial & Infrastructure segment. The Oil & Gas segment also contributed to the revenue decline.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating in key sectors, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Backlog orders are growing, with the Oil & Gas segment contributing significantly to this increase.

evidence.text: Consolidated backlog as of June 30, 2015 was $41.6 billion compared to $40.3 billion as of June 30, 2014. The increase in backlog was primarily due to new awards booked after the second quarter of 2014 in the Oil & Gas segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Backlog orders are growing, particularly in the Oil & Gas segment, signaling strong demand.

factors.summary: The company is facing pressure on margins due to intense competition, particularly in the Oil & Gas and Industrial & Infrastructure segments.

evidence.text: The company is still experiencing a highly competitive business environment, with pressure on margins. In some cases, margins may be favorably or unfavorably impacted by a change in the mix of work performed or a change in the amount of customer-furnished materials, which are accounted for as pass-through costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pressure on margins due to competition suggests a moderate challenge.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, such as engineering activities, particularly in the Oil & Gas segment.

evidence.text: The Oil & Gas segment experienced higher segment profit margin that was partially due to a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin activities improves profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with declines in the Industrial & Infrastructure and Power segments offsetting growth in the Oil & Gas segment.

evidence.text: Consolidated revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2015 decreased 8 percent from $5.3 billion to $4.8 billion compared to the three months ended June 30, 2014. The revenue decreases in the current year periods were principally due to a significant decline in project execution activities in the mining and metals and infrastructure business lines of the Industrial & Infrastructure segment.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decelerating growth across multiple segments points to a slowdown.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with declines in certain segments (mining and metals) offset by increases in others (Oil & Gas engineering activities).

evidence.text: The Oil & Gas segment experienced higher segment profit margin that was partially due to a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends with some declines and increases.

factors.summary: The Oil & Gas end market is showing strength, while the mining and metals sector is slowing due to reduced capital investment.

evidence.text: The Oil & Gas segment has continued to show strength, but declining oil prices since the latter part of 2014 could affect the segment's current projects and the timing of new awards. In the Industrial & Infrastructure segment, mining and metals business has continued to slow as major capital investment decisions by most mining customers have been deferred.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Strength in Oil & Gas is offset by slowing mining and metals sectors.

factors.summary: Input costs are not explicitly mentioned as rising or declining, but the company is managing costs through efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: The company is experiencing a highly competitive business environment with pressure on margins, but specific input cost trends are not detailed.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant mention of wage inflation or benefit expenses creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: The company is experiencing a highly competitive business environment with pressure on margins, but specific labor cost trends are not detailed.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with no significant mention of wage inflation or margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving in the Oil & Gas segment but declining in the Industrial & Infrastructure and Power segments.

evidence.text: Segment profit margin for the three and six months ended June 30, 2015 increased to 7.4 percent compared to 5.9 percent and 5.4 percent, respectively, for the corresponding periods in 2014. These improvements were largely attributable to the positive effect of forecast revisions associated with the reassessment of risk for certain projects progressing towards completion, a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities and a reduction in segment overhead expenses in the current year resulting from cost optimization activities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins in the Oil & Gas segment despite declines elsewhere.

factors.summary: Cost reduction and efficiency programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in the Oil & Gas and Power segments.

evidence.text: The company is still experiencing a highly competitive business environment, with pressure on margins. In some cases, margins may be favorably or unfavorably impacted by a change in the mix of work performed or a change in the amount of customer-furnished materials, which are accounted for as pass-through costs. During the first six months of 2015, the Oil & Gas segment experienced higher segment profit margin that was partially due to a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities. This shift corresponds to an increase in the volume of project execution activities for projects that are in the earlier stages of the project life cycle compared to a year ago.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly due to cost optimization activities in the Oil & Gas and Power segments.

evidence.text: The company is still experiencing a highly competitive business environment, with pressure on margins. In some cases, margins may be favorably or unfavorably impacted by a change in the mix of work performed or a change in the amount of customer-furnished materials, which are accounted for as pass-through costs. During the first six months of 2015, the Oil & Gas segment experienced higher segment profit margin that was partially due to a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities. This shift corresponds to an increase in the volume of project execution activities for projects that are in the earlier stages of the project life cycle compared to a year ago.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements from cost optimization activities.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are not explicitly detailed, but the company is maintaining its capital structure with a conservative financial strategy.

evidence.text: The company has committed and uncommitted lines of credit totaling $5.4 billion, which may be used for revolving loans, letters of credit and/or general purposes. The company believes that for at least the next 12 months, cash generated from operations, along with its unused credit capacity of $4.0 billion and substantial cash position, is sufficient to support operating requirements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No explicit details on capital expenditures, but maintaining capital structure.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with a debt-to-capital ratio improving slightly from 24.7% to 24.2%.

evidence.text: The company's total debt to total capitalization (debt-to-capital) ratio as of June 30, 2015 was 24.2 percent compared to 24.7 percent as of December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Debt-to-capital ratio improved slightly, indicating deleveraging.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its buyback program, with a focus on returning cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Quarterly cash dividends are typically paid during the month following the quarter in which they are declared. Therefore, dividends declared in the fourth quarter of 2014 were paid in the first quarter of 2015. Quarterly cash dividends of $0.21 per share were declared in the second quarter of 2015 and 2014. The payment and level of future cash dividends is subject to the discretion of the company's Board of Directors.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining buyback program but no significant changes in dividend levels.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with cash flows from operating activities increasing, though still lower than prior years due to certain outflows.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $204 million for the six months ended June 30, 2015 compared to $426 million for the corresponding period of the prior year. The period-over-period decrease in cash flows from operating activities was primarily attributable to cash outflows totaling $306 million associated with discontinued operations as discussed below.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Cash flow from operations decreased due to outflows from discontinued operations.

factors.summary: The company is not actively pursuing significant M&A activity, with recent activity limited to the sale of interests in joint ventures.

evidence.text: During the first half of 2014, the company sold its interest in two joint ventures in the Industrial & Infrastructure segment for $44 million. The company had a 10 percent interest in both joint ventures and accounted for these investments using the equity method.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company's M&A activity is limited to selling interests, indicating no significant expansion or contraction.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance, but cautionary statements indicate uncertainty due to various risks.

evidence.text: Any forward-looking statements that we may make are based on our current expectations and beliefs concerning future developments and their potential effects on us. There can be no assurance that future developments affecting us will be those anticipated by us. Any forward-looking statements are subject to the risks, uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual results of operations, financial condition, cost reductions, acquisitions, dispositions, financing transactions, operations, expansion, consolidation and other events to differ materially from those expressed or implied in such forward-looking statements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management has not explicitly changed guidance but has expressed caution, suggesting uncertainty.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to the mention of various risks and uncertainties, though they maintain a conservative financial strategy to preserve liquidity.

evidence.text: The company cannot control such risk factors and other uncertainties, and in many cases, cannot predict the risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those indicated by the forward-looking statements. These risks and uncertainties should be considered when evaluating the company and deciding whether to invest in its securities. Except as otherwise required by law, the company undertakes no obligation to publicly update or revise its forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious due to risks but maintains a conservative strategy, showing stability.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include declining oil prices affecting the Oil & Gas segment, reduced mining investments, and competitive pressures.

evidence.text: Declining oil prices since the latter part of 2014 could affect the segment's current projects and the timing of new awards. In the Industrial & Infrastructure segment, mining and metals business has continued to slow as major capital investment decisions by most mining customers have been deferred.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include negative impacts from declining oil prices and reduced mining investments.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on maintaining a strong balance sheet and conservative financial strategy to navigate through market uncertainties.

evidence.text: The company's conservative financial strategy and consistent performance have earned it strong credit ratings, resulting in competitive advantage and continued access to the capital markets. As of June 30, 2015, the company was in compliance with all its covenants related to its debt agreements. The company's total debt to total capitalization (debt-to-capital) ratio as of June 30, 2015 was 24.2 percent compared to 24.7 percent as of December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company's long-term strategy focuses on financial stability, which is prudent but not indicating significant positive or negative movement.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include oil prices, foreign exchange rates, and economic conditions affecting clients and partners.

evidence.text: Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: · The cyclical nature of many of the markets the company serves, including our commodity-based business lines, and our vulnerability to downturns; · Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company's clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers; · Changes in global business, economic (including currency risk), political and social conditions; · Foreign exchange risks;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company faces various sensitivities, but these are standard risks without clear positive or negative signals.

factors.summary: Catalysts include the timing of new awards in the Oil & Gas and Industrial & Infrastructure segments, as well as the outcome of ongoing projects and contracts.

evidence.text: The Oil & Gas segment has continued to show strength, but declining oil prices since the latter part of 2014 could affect the segment's current projects and the timing of new awards. In the Industrial & Infrastructure segment, mining and metals business has continued to slow as major capital investment decisions by most mining customers have been deferred.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Catalysts include project timing and outcomes, which are uncertain and do not provide a clear sentiment direction.
